commands.review_summary:
The branch adds session-title helpers, changes `/name` to mirror a new `title` field to legacy `label`, adds `/name --clear`, and adds focused helper tests.

PR surface: Source +69, Tests +42. Total +111 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: not applicable. as a bug reproduction: this is a feature/migration PR. Source inspection is enough to show current main uses `label` consistently and the branch starts a partial `title` migration without real runtime proof.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Maintainer review is needed for the canonical session naming/data-model direction, and the contributor must add real behavior proof; no narrow automation repair is safe yet.

target.title: fix(sessions): start canonical session title migration
target.body:
## Summary

- Adds canonical session title helpers next to the existing label parser while preserving the legacy label API.
- Updates `/name` to write a canonical `title` and mirror it to legacy `label` for compatibility.
- Adds `/name --clear` so slash-command clearing matches the API clear semantics.
- Adds focused tests for title parsing, legacy label compatibility, title/label mirroring, clearing, and normalized comparisons.

## Scope

This is the first compatibility step of the session naming cleanup. It intentionally does not yet route `sessions.patch`, `sessions.resolve`, gateway row projection, or transcript `session_info.name` through one complete rename service. Those should be follow-up work once the helper layer is accepted.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed: `/name` no longer writes only the legacy label field. It now writes a canonical title and mirrors it to label so existing selectors and UI paths continue to work. `/name --clear` clears both fields.

Real environment tested: Not run locally from this ChatGPT session. GitHub edits were made through the connector.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
# Not run in this environment
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/sessions/session-title.test.ts
```

Observed result after fix: Not locally verified here. The branch contains focused Vitest coverage for the new helpers.

What was not tested: Gateway patch/unification, session resolve by label/title, browser UI, TUI refresh, transcript session_info migration, and full OpenClaw runtime behavior.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? Yes. `/name --clear` is added, and `/name <title>` now mirrors a title field to the legacy label field.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? No.

What is the highest-risk area? Mixed title/label compatibility while gateway patch and resolve still operate on label.

How is that risk mitigated? The slash command mirrors title to label and all existing legacy label reads continue to work.

AI-assisted changes: Yes, generated with ChatGPT.
target.author: jason-allen-oneal
